crime.value: Public suicide of a wealthy student, later connected to murder; central question: are these deaths connected and part of a larger conspiracy?
crime.quote: The bizarre and tragic suicide is soon connected to a clear case of murder, witnessed firsthand by Fandorin himself.

victim.value: A talented student from a wealthy family who shoots himself in public; later a murder victim whose death connects to the suicide; also Fandorin himself becomes a target of assassination attempts.
victim.quote: What would cause a talented student from a wealthy family to shoot himself in front of a promenading public?

closed_world.value: Moscow in 1876, expanding across Europe; initially bounded by Alexander Gardens public promenade and hotel system; conspiracy spans diplomatic and international networks.
closed_world.quote: Moscow, May 1876... leads him across Europe, landing him at the center of a vast conspiracy

culprit_and_motive.value: An international conspiracy (Amalia Bezhetskaya and associates) with operatives in the Russian embassy; motive involves silencing Fandorin and controlling information.
culprit_and_motive.quote: They had their own people in the Russian embassy!... 'Don't you dare! It's my fault, and I shall correct my own mistake.'

investigator.value: Erast Fandorin, a young, talented detective dissatisfied with official conclusions; pursues investigation through keen intuition despite institutional resistance.
investigator.quote: young sleuth Erast Fandorin is not satisfied with the conclusion... Relying on his keen intuition, the eager detective plunges into an investigation
